title: salix × lloydii
intro: Salix × lloydii, also known as the Black Willow or Weeping Willow, is a hybrid deciduous tree that belongs to the Salicaceae family, resulting from a cross between the Salix × sepulcralis and Salix alba species. The bark and leaves of the Salix × lloydii plant have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help to treat conditions such as arthritis, gout, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Salix × lloydii include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and supporting digestive health. Salix × lloydii can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
